title: Shiva Ashtakam
description: Eight verses in praise of Lord Shiva. A beloved Shaiva hymn recited at dawn.
source: Traditional Sanskrit, public domain. English translations are original paraphrases by the Divya Granth project.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रभुं प्राणनाथं विभुं विश्वनाथं जगन्नाथनाथं सदानन्दभाजम्।
भवद्भव्यभूतेश्वरं भूतनाथं शिवं शङ्करं शम्भुमीशानमीडे॥
verses.transliteration:
prabhuṃ prāṇanāthaṃ vibhuṃ viśvanāthaṃ jagannātha-nāthaṃ sadānanda-bhājam.
bhavad-bhavya-bhūteśvaraṃ bhūta-nāthaṃ śivaṃ śaṅkaraṃ śambhum īśānam īḍe.

translations.en: I praise the Lord, master of life-breath, all-pervading lord of the universe, master of the master of the world, ever drinking bliss; lord of all that has been, will be, and is, lord of beings — Shiva, Shankara, Shambhu, Ishana.

verses.sanskrit:
गले रुण्डमालं तनौ सर्पजालं महाकालकालं गणेशादिपालम्।
जटाजूटगङ्गोत्तरङ्गैर्विशालं शिवं शङ्करं शम्भुमीशानमीडे॥
verses.transliteration:
gale ruṇḍa-mālaṃ tanau sarpa-jālaṃ mahākāla-kālaṃ gaṇeśādi-pālam.
jaṭā-jūṭa-gaṅgottaraṅgair viśālaṃ śivaṃ śaṅkaraṃ śambhum īśānam īḍe.

translations.en: With a garland of skulls on his neck, a net of serpents on his body — death even of Mahakala, protector of Ganesha and others; vast with the rolling waves of the Ganga in his matted hair — Shiva, Shankara, Shambhu, Ishana, I praise.

verses.sanskrit:
मुदामाकरं मण्डनं मण्डयन्तं महामण्डलं भस्मभूषाधरं तम्।
अनादिह्यपारं महामोहहारं शिवं शङ्करं शम्भुमीशानमीडे॥
verses.transliteration:
mudām ākaraṃ maṇḍanaṃ maṇḍayantaṃ mahā-maṇḍalaṃ bhasma-bhūṣādharaṃ tam.
anādi hy apāraṃ mahā-moha-hāraṃ śivaṃ śaṅkaraṃ śambhum īśānam īḍe.

translations.en: Source of joy, ornament adorning the great cosmic circle, wearing ash as his ornament — beginningless, boundless, dispeller of great delusion — Shiva, Shankara, Shambhu, Ishana, I praise.

verses.sanskrit:
वटाधोनिवासं महाट्टाट्टहासं महापापनाशं सदा सुप्रकाशम्।
गिरीशं गणेशं सुरेशं महेशं शिवं शङ्करं शम्भुमीशानमीडे॥
verses.transliteration:
vaṭādho-nivāsaṃ mahāṭṭāṭṭa-hāsaṃ mahā-pāpa-nāśaṃ sadā suprakāśam.
girīśaṃ gaṇeśaṃ sureśaṃ maheśaṃ śivaṃ śaṅkaraṃ śambhum īśānam īḍe.

translations.en: Dwelling beneath the banyan, with great roaring laughter, destroyer of great sin, ever radiant; lord of mountains, lord of the ganas, lord of the gods, the great lord — Shiva, Shankara, Shambhu, Ishana, I praise.

verses.sanskrit:
गिरीन्द्रात्मजासङ्गृहीतार्धदेहं गिरौ संस्थितं सर्वदाऽपन्नगेहम्।
परब्रह्मब्रह्मादिभिर्वन्द्यमानं शिवं शङ्करं शम्भुमीशानमीडे॥
verses.transliteration:
girīndrātmajā-saṅgṛhītārdha-dehaṃ girau saṃsthitaṃ sarvadā'pannagehaṃ.
parabrahma-brahmādibhir vandyamānaṃ śivaṃ śaṅkaraṃ śambhum īśānam īḍe.

translations.en: Whose half-body is held by the daughter of the mountain-king, dwelling on the mountain, his home for refuge always; worshipped by Brahma and the supreme Brahman itself — Shiva, Shankara, Shambhu, Ishana, I praise.

verses.sanskrit:
कपालं त्रिशूलं कराभ्यां दधानं पदाम्भोजनम्राय कामं ददानम्।
बलीवर्दयानं सुराणां प्रधानं शिवं शङ्करं शम्भुमीशानमीडे॥
verses.transliteration:
kapālaṃ triśūlaṃ karābhyāṃ dadhānaṃ padāmbhoja-namrāya kāmaṃ dadānam.
balīvarda-yānaṃ surāṇāṃ pradhānaṃ śivaṃ śaṅkaraṃ śambhum īśānam īḍe.

translations.en: Holding skull and trident in his hands, granting all desires to those who bow at his lotus feet; riding on the bull, foremost among the gods — Shiva, Shankara, Shambhu, Ishana, I praise.

verses.sanskrit:
शरच्चन्द्रगात्रं गणानन्दपात्रं त्रिनेत्रं पवित्रं धनेशस्य मित्रम्।
अपर्णाकलत्रं चरित्रं विचित्रं शिवं शङ्करं शम्भुमीशानमीडे॥
verses.transliteration:
śarac-candra-gātraṃ gaṇānanda-pātraṃ trinetraṃ pavitraṃ dhaneśasya mitram.
aparṇā-kalatraṃ caritraṃ vicitraṃ śivaṃ śaṅkaraṃ śambhum īśānam īḍe.

translations.en: His body bright as the autumn moon, vessel of the ganas' joy, three-eyed, pure, friend of Kubera; husband of Aparna, of wondrous deeds — Shiva, Shankara, Shambhu, Ishana, I praise.

verses.sanskrit:
हरं सर्पहारं चिताभूविहारं भवं वेदसारं सदा निर्विकारम्।
श्मशाने वसन्तं मनोजं दहन्तं शिवं शङ्करं शम्भुमीशानमीडे॥
verses.transliteration:
haraṃ sarpa-hāraṃ citā-bhū-vihāraṃ bhavaṃ veda-sāraṃ sadā nirvikāram.
śmaśāne vasantaṃ manojaṃ dahantaṃ śivaṃ śaṅkaraṃ śambhum īśānam īḍe.

translations.en: Hara, wearing serpents as garlands, who roams the cremation ground; existence itself, essence of the Vedas, ever changeless; dwelling in the burning ground, who burned the god of love — Shiva, Shankara, Shambhu, Ishana, I praise.
